---
title: "Resources Reviewed"
---

Every book, course, paper, and webinar I've worked through, in one place. Rating is personal — how useful *for my goals* on a 1–5 scale, not an objective quality judgment.

## Books

| Resource | Status | Rating | One-line take | Detailed notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Hernán & Robins, *Causal Inference: What If* | In progress (Pt. I) | — | The reference for causal thinking; dense but worth it. | [Ch. 1](../methods-concepts/what-if-ch1-causal-questions.qmd) |

## Courses

| Resource | Status | Rating | One-line take | Detailed notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| ISPOR Short Course — Causal Inference (May 17, 2026) | Upcoming | — | — | — |
| ISPOR Short Course — Developing Decision-Grade RWE (May 17, 2026) | Upcoming | — | — | — |
| WUSS — AI for biometrics & agentic workflows | Enrolled | — | — | — |
| WUSS — RECIST to Kaplan-Meier oncology endpoints | Enrolled | — | — | — |

## Papers

*To be added as I read them.*

## Webinars / Talks

*To be added.*

---

::: {.callout-tip}
**My rating scale**

- **5** — Changed how I think about a topic. Will re-read.
- **4** — Strong, recommended; I learned a lot.
- **3** — Useful for what it is; reference-grade.
- **2** — Mixed; some good parts.
- **1** — Skip.
:::
